chunks.en: Today I want to talk about how I write commit messages in my daily work.
chunks.ja: 今日は、毎日の仕事でコミットメッセージをどう書いているかについて話したいです。

chunks.en: I usually commit at small, clean points, like right after I finish one function.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい区切りのいい小さなところ、たとえば一つの関数を書き終えた直後にコミットします。

chunks.en: The words I put in the message are usually simple ones like 'fix', 'add', or 'update'.
chunks.ja: メッセージに入れる言葉は、たいてい「fix」「add」「update」のような簡単なものです。

chunks.en: When my message gets too vague, like 'various changes', I use amend to rewrite it clearly.
chunks.ja: 「いろいろ修正」のようにメッセージが雑になりすぎたときは、amend を使ってはっきり書き直します。

outline: Opening: how I write commit messages
Point 1: when I commit and the words I use
Point 2: fixing vague messages with amend
Wrap-up: keeping messages small and clear

modelTalk.text: I write commit messages a lot, so let me tell you how I do it. I like to commit small and often. When I finish one clear thing, I stop and commit right away. I don't wait until the end of the day. For the words, I keep it simple. I start with "fix" when I repair a bug, or "add" when I make something new. Sometimes "update" too. But when I'm tired, my message gets lazy. I write things like "fix stuff" or "various changes." That's bad, because later I can't remember what I did. When I catch a typo or a weak message, I just fix it. If it's the last commit, I use "amend" and write it again. It feels much cleaner after that. Good messages really help me later. So I try hard to keep them clear.

chunks.en: Today I want to talk about how I write my Git commit messages.
chunks.ja: 今日は、私がGitのコミットメッセージをどう書いているかについて話したいです。

chunks.en: I usually commit in small pieces whenever I finish one clear step.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい、区切りのいい一つの作業が終わるたびに小さくコミットします。

chunks.en: The words I put in the message are simple ones like "fix" or "add".
chunks.ja: メッセージに入れる言葉は、「fix」や「add」みたいなシンプルなものです。

chunks.en: When I notice a typo, I use amend to fix the bad message right away.
chunks.ja: タイプミスに気づいたら、amendを使ってすぐに雑なメッセージを直します。

outline: Opening: how I write commit messages
When and how small I commit
The starter words I always use
Wrap-up: fixing vague or bad messages

modelTalk.text:
I don't wait until the end of my work to commit. I like to stop at good points and make small commits. That way, each change stays clear. It's easier to go back if something breaks.

For the words, I keep them simple. I usually start with "fix" when I repair a bug. I write "add" when I put in something new. Sometimes I use "update" too.

But I'm not always careful. When I'm tired, my message gets vague. I just write "fix stuff" or "some changes." Later, I look back and I can't remember what I did. That's a bad habit.

So how do I fix a bad message? Sometimes I spot a typo right away. Then I use "amend" to redo the last one. It's quick, and it keeps my history clean and honest.
